assertions.text: Vehicle was traveling east at just under 100 km/h.
Driver lost control on the curve before/braking or during; specifically: loss of control occurred on the curve prior to leaving the road.
Driver braked hard for several seconds before the car left the road.
Car left the road (departed the roadway) during the event.
Car impacted a tree head-on (frontal collision with a tree).
There was one impact (the vehicle struck a tree once).
Collision was severe enough to be described as the car hitting a tree head-on (implying significant impact).

verdicts.evidence: The pre-crash speed samples show the vehicle speed was [98.0](T2) km/h at t=[-5.0](T1) s and [92.0](T2) km/h at t=[0.0](T1) s, which supports the claim of traveling just under [100](T2) km/h.

Telemetry shows pre-crash speed samples and the crash pulse but lacks a dedicated 'loss of control' signal or curve location data; there is no sensor channel indicating road curvature or skid before departure, so the timing/location of 'loss of control on the curve prior to leaving the road' is [unverifiable](T1).

Brake channel samples show the brake was off for all pre-crash samples (fraction_on=[0.0](T2)) and no transitions were recorded, so the claim that the driver 'braked hard for several seconds before' is contradicted by the telemetry (no brake application recorded) as shown in the brake statistics [fraction_on=0.0](T2).

The EDR summary indicates a crash pulse with a large longitudinal delta-V (final cumulative longitudinal change=[-92.0](T2) km/h and peak value=[-95.0](T1) km/h at t=[125.0](T1) ms) consistent with a departure from driving followed by a single severe impact; while 'left the road' is not a direct sensor channel, the high longitudinal delta-V and single-vehicle event (vehicles_in_crash=[1](T1)) support a roadway departure and collision with a fixed object during the event.

Sensors record a dominant longitudinal delta-V (value_at_max=[-95.0](T2) km/h) indicating a large front/rear-directed velocity change, but device axes orientation relative to vehicle front is undocumented; therefore whether this corresponds to a frontal impact with a tree is [unverifiable](T1).

EDR summary reports num_events_recorded=[1](T1) and the crash pulse shows a single dominant longitudinal delta-V with final value=[-92.0](T2) km/h, supporting that there was one impact recorded for V1.

The crash pulse shows a large longitudinal delta-V peak of [95.0](T1) km/h (value_at_max=[-95.0](T2) km/h) and a final cumulative longitudinal change of [-92.0](T2) km/h, which indicate a severe impact consistent with the narrative that the car hit a tree head-on.

result.rationale: Telemetry clearly shows the vehicle was near [98.0](T2)–[92.0](T2) km/h pre-crash, no brake application in pre-crash samples ([fraction_on=0.0](T2)), and a large single crash pulse (num_events_recorded=[1](T1), peak delta-V=[-95.0](T1)). However, road-curve loss-of-control and impact object orientation (head-on with a tree) are not determinable from the available device-frame sensor data, making some assertions unverifiable.

result.time_zero: impact (t=0); pre-crash negative, crash pulse positive

channels.name: delta_v_long
channels.desc: Cumulative longitudinal delta-V during impact (times converted from ms to s)

channels.name: delta_v_lat
channels.desc: Cumulative lateral delta-V during impact (times converted from ms to s)

result.notes: NHTSA CISS EDR data: sparse pre-crash series (~2 Hz, t<0) plus a crash-pulse delta-V curve (t>=0, ~10 ms steps). t=0 is the impact.

delta_v_long.note: cumulative velocity change during the crash pulse

delta_v_lat.note: cumulative velocity change during the crash pulse
